purpose: Define the source-owner handoff blocks required before Brand Strategic Context can be treated as official brand strategy for executive or agency-facing use.

requirements.label: Approved Brand Strategic Context source
requirements.sourceOwnerRole: Marketing, Insights, or Brand Strategy source owner
validationRules: brandId must match the active Brand Health Record
sourceDate must be present and traceable
sourceOwner must identify the accountable owner
requirements.canonicalUseCondition: Only source-owner approved Brand Strategic Context packets can be treated as official brand strategy.
guardrails: Draft, prototype, browser-local, or reviewed-local packets cannot satisfy this gate.
Approved-looking local packets remain review candidates until promoted through governed source ownership.
requirements.starterQuestion: Who is the accountable source owner for the official brand book, brand DNA, or strategy brief?

requirements.label: Brand foundations and DNA
requirements.sourceOwnerRole: Brand Marketing / Brand Strategy source owner
validationRules: brandStatement must be a source-provided statement, not inferred from BBE metrics
brandDna must contain explicit source-provided foundations
portfolioContext must be caveated if it comes from prototype or planning assumptions
requirements.canonicalUseCondition: Brand foundations can be used as official strategy only when source-owner approved fields are loaded.
guardrails: Do not infer brand DNA from demand power, SMD, pricing power, or typology.
Do not turn portfolio role metadata into official brand strategy without source approval.
requirements.starterQuestion: Which approved source owns the brand statement, DNA, and portfolio role for this planning period?

requirements.label: Positioning, objectives, and planning priorities
validationRules: positioning must be source-provided when used in executive or agency-facing outputs
objectives must be tied to an approved annual planning or strategy source
planning priorities must stay distinct from diagnostic recommendations
requirements.canonicalUseCondition: Positioning, objectives, and planning priorities can guide generated workspaces only after approved source-owner review.
guardrails: If positioning is missing, do not infer it from equity diagnosis.
If objectives are missing, present questions and gaps rather than strategic intent.
requirements.starterQuestion: Which approved planning or strategy source owns positioning, annual objectives, and current priorities?

requirements.label: Creative platform and approved claims
requirements.sourceOwnerRole: Brand Marketing / Creative or Legal Review owner
validationRules: creativePlatform must be source-provided before agency brief language can cite it
approvedClaims must list allowed claims or explicitly state none are approved
claimsNotToMake must include known claim boundaries when available
requirements.canonicalUseCondition: Creative platform and claim language remain human-review drafts until approved source fields and claim boundaries are loaded.
guardrails: Do not invent campaign platforms or claims from BBE evidence.
Do not present treatment language as approved claim language.
requirements.starterQuestion: Which approved creative brief, brand book, or claim-review source owns platform language and claim boundaries?

guardrails: The handoff registry is a source-owner checklist, not permission to write canonical brand strategy automatically.
Brand Strategic Context may guide generated workspaces only at the status explicitly supported by its source packet and review state.
Canonical source writes, runtime auto-consumption, and enterprise strategy promotion remain disabled until governance enables them.
caveats: This registry defines the official-context handoff contract; it does not prove that official brand sources exist.
Prototype-reviewed context can support demo behavior, but must remain visibly caveated in executive or agency-facing experiences.
